_comment: Multi-GPU split profiles for ELI. Set ONE profile's "enabled": true to split a single large model across multiple GPUs (e.g. a 70B+ or trillion-param model that doesn't fit one card). All-disabled (default) = single-GPU, no change. Only applied when 2+ GPUs are actually present. tensor_split = per-GPU weights (must sum to ~1.0, one entry per GPU); split_mode = none|layer|row; main_gpu = primary device index. Fully local — this only controls how a model is placed across YOUR GPUs. Copy to config/gpu_profiles.json or point ELI_GPU_PROFILES_FILE at a custom file.

profiles.name: dual-gpu-equal
profiles.description: Split a model evenly across 2 GPUs.

profiles.name: dual-gpu-weighted
profiles.description: Uneven split when one GPU has more VRAM / is faster.

profiles.name: eight-gpu-equal
profiles.description: Even split across 8 GPUs (datacenter server for very large models).
